text: Hi everyone, this video demonstrates some tips and tricks for doing a low anterior resection in an obese patient. This patient has a rectal cancer, eleven centimetres from the anal verge. Doing a laparoscopic colorectal resection in an obese patient is challenging. the oncological profile of the operation should be of prime importance. It's therefore vital that the vessel ligation and dissection of the TME plane should never be compromised. As demonstrated here, the mesocolon and the mesentery is quite fat-laden. Traction on the pedicle of the inferior mesenteric artery is very important. In large patients, we routinely start our dissection from the sacral promontory and work our way up to the top of the TME plane. This particular trick helps to identify the ureter as would be demonstrated later during the video and also develop a plane underneath the inferior mesenteric artery and vein. Oncology of this procedure should never be compromised although it's done laparoscopically. It's therefore important to do a high ligation and dissect the IMA as close to its origin from the aorta as possible.

The surgeon dissects and frees the top of the total mesorectal excision (TME) plane, keeping the inferior mesenteric artery (IMA) taut to facilitate its skeletonization. The IMA is dissected up to its origin from the aorta, with the ureter clearly visible and preserved. A plane is created between the inferior mesenteric artery and vein, separating them from the overlying fatty tissue.

The surgeon manipulates the instrument to push toward the opposite side of the bulky inferior mesenteric artery (IMA) and inferior mesenteric vein (IMV) pedicle, tautening the vascular pedicle and revealing the ureter from the opposite side.
The surgeon visually identifies the origin of the inferior mesenteric artery (IMA) from the aorta, highlighting the anatomical relationship between these structures.
In part two of this video we will demonstrate the dissection of the TME plane and a nerve preserving low anterior dissection. Many thanks for watching.
